description.p: As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 necrotic damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique. This technique's damage increases by 1d8 when you reach 5th level (2d8), 11th level (3d8), and 17th level (4d8).

sheet.description: As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 necrotic damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 necrotic damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 necrotic damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 4d8 necrotic damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

description.p: As an action, you can target one creature within 15ft. and teleport behind them making a weapon attack roll against the creature. Immediately afterwards, teleport behind a different creature within 15ft. and make a weapon attack roll against them.

sheet.description: Target one creature within 15ft. and teleport behind them making a weapon attack roll against the creature. Immediately afterwards, teleport behind a different creature within 15ft. and make a weapon attack roll against them.

description.p: As an action, you charge forwards in a 20ft. line that is 5ft. wide, using 10ft. of movement and causing all enemies to take 2d4 Bludgeoning damage and make a Strength Saving Throw or be knocked prone, the DC = 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Strength Modifier.

sheet.description: charge forwards in a 20ft. line that is 5ft. wide, using 10ft. of movement and causing all enemies to take 2d4 Bludgeoning damage and make a DC {{battering_ram:dc}} Strength Saving Throw or be knocked prone.

description.p: Gain resistance to Necrotic Damage. When you succeed a saving throw induced by a spells effect, if you would still take damage, you resist it. You are considered proficient with this armor even if you lack proficiency with heavy armor, and do not suffer a stealth disadvantage for wearing it.

Curse. This armor is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by the remove curse spell at 9th level or similar magic. Removing the armor fails to end the curse on you. Whenever you are downed by radiant damage, you become vulnerable to all damage sources until stabilized.

Gain resistance to Radiant Damage. When you succeed a saving throw induced by a spells effect, if you would still take damage, you resist it. You are considered proficient with this armor even if you lack proficiency with heavy armor, and do not suffer a stealth disadvantage for wearing it.

While holding this shield, you have a bonus to AC determined by the shield's rarity. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. You also gain resistance to non-magical bludgeoning damage. When targeted by a projectile spell attack, if the attack were to miss, it is instead absorbed, restoring 1d4 health, increasing by 1d4 per spell level absorbed. (1st lvl spell heals 1d4, 2nd lvl spell heals 2d4, etc.)

Curse. This shield is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by the remove curse spell at 9th level or similar magic. Removing the shield fails to end the curse on you. Whenever a creature is killed by you within 5ft. you are bombarded with visions of Valik Millithor's regrets, and have disadvantage on all attack rolls until the end of your next turn.

While holding this shield, you have a bonus to AC determined by the shield's rarity. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. You also gain resistance to piercing damage. While being flanked, enemies do not get advantage when attacking you.

Said to be the gift of a mysterious god of the night, this mystical robe can change shape and size according to the wearer's desires, from a tiny bracelet to an extravagant ball gown size, the robe is always seemingly made of pure night sky.
While wearing these garments, you have immunity to damage from the magic missile and crown of stars spells.

This ominous armor was obviously crafted by someone with an affinity for the undead. You gain a +3 bonus to AC while attuned to this magic armor. Additionally, you are immune to the poisoned condition and have immunity to poison damage. This armor can only be attuned by spellcasters.
Legendary Resistance (1/day): While attuned to this armor, if you fail a saving throw you can choose to succeed instead.
From Power, Death (1/day): When you roll damage for any spell you cast, you can add an amount of necrotic damage equal to half of your current hitpoints.

From Life, Power: When you cast a spell, you can choose to expend hit points instead of a spell slot. When you do so, roll a number of d4s equal to the spell's level. Lower your total maximum hit points by an amount equal to the roll. Lost maximum hit points are recovered on completion of a long rest. If this effect would lower your maximum hitpoints to 0, the spell fails to cast.

Curse. This armor is cursed and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. While cursed, you have disadvantage on death saving throws. If you die while attuned to this armor you cannot be resurrected by any means other than a wish spell. Additionally you arise as a zombie in 1d4 hours after death.

Scales of the Trout: This armor does not weigh you down in water and even makes you a more potent swimmer. Your movement speed when swimming is not halved. Additionally you make Strength (Athletics) checks for swimming with advantage.

This shield is embossed with personified sun and moon designs. The sun's eyes magically open between dawn and dusk, and the moon's eyes open between dusk and dawn. While holding the shield, you can use an action to cause the sheild to shed bright light in a 20-foot radius, and dim light for an additional 20 feet. The light is warmly colored while the sun's eyes are open, but cool while the moon's are. The light remains for 1 hour or until you use an action to end it.

This armor holds a number of charges equal to twice its AC bonus and regain all expended charges each day at dusk. Once per turn when you hit a creature which has blood with piercing or slashing damage while wearing this armor, you can expend 1 charge and inflict an additional 2d6 of your weapon's damage as the armor absorbs athe blood of your target. You then gain temporary hit points equal to the piercing or slashing damage received, as the armor is empowered and grows temporary parts from the blood it absorbed.

This armor holds a number of charges equal to twice its AC bonus and regain all expended charges each day at dusk. Once per turn when you hit a creature which has blood with piercing or slashing damage while wearing this armor, you can expend 1 charge and inflict an additional 2d6 of your weapon's damage as the armor absorbs athe blood of your target. You then gain temporary hit points equal to the piercing or slashing damage received, as the armor is empowered and grows temporary parts from the blood it absorbed.

This armor holds a number of charges equal to twice its AC bonus and regain all expended charges each day at dusk. Once per turn when you hit a creature which has blood with piercing or slashing damage while wearing this armor, you can expend 1 charge and inflict an additional 2d6 of your weapon's damage as the armor absorbs athe blood of your target. You then gain temporary hit points equal to the piercing or slashing damage received, as the armor is empowered and grows temporary parts from the blood it absorbed.
The axe of a legendary barbarian native to the Mystlands. In their final moments cursing the axe with the power of their feller, before an unknown tragedy.
Attacks made with this weapon deal an additional 1d4 Lightning Damage and stack a charge of Lightning.
When making an attack with this weapon when it has 3 charges of lightning, deal an additional 1d10 Lightning damage to the target and 3 creatures of choice within 15ft. consuming the charges on use.
This weapons attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistances.
When dealing damage to an enemy, add an extra 1d4 Fire Damage and 1d4 Cold Damage to the damage roll.
When felling a foe with this weapon, the wielder rekindles a brief amount of life force, regaining 1d4 health and removing a level of exhaustion.
Enemies felled by this blade cannot be resurrected without the use of divine intervention.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and deal an additional 4d6 Necrotic damage upon striking a target. You also gain access to Bounding Necrosis, which can be used twice per long rest.

Upon landing a critical strike, you may use your reaction to take advantage of your opponents sudden affliction to make another attack roll against the target. When critically striking an opponent, heal 3d8 hitpoints.

Curse. This sword is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by the remove curse spell at 9th level or similar magic. Dropping the sword fails to end the curse on you. Whenever you kill a creature with this blade, you kill a part of your own self, after killing 1000 enemies with this blade, your allignment finally becomes chaotic evil and remains as such even if this curse is removed.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and deal an additional 4d6 Radiant damage upon striking a target. You also gain access to Bounding Radiance, which can be used twice per long rest.

Upon landing a critical strike, you may use your reaction to take advantage of your opponents sudden affliction to make another attack roll against the target. When critically striking an opponent, heal 3d8 hitpoints.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 radiant damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique. This technique's damage increases by 1d8 when you reach 5th level (2d8), 11th level (3d8), and 17th level (4d8).

sheet.description: As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 radiant damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 radiant damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 radiant damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

As an action, you can propel a blade of arcane energy from Vile Sting at a creature within 15ft. Make an attack roll against the target. On a hit, the target takes 4d8 radiant damage. The blade then leaps to a different target of your choice within 15ft. of the original target. Make a new attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. On a hit, the blade can continue leaping to a third target. A creature can only be targeted once by each use of this technique.

description.p: A ring once worn by the high elf known as Dianne, to symbolize her love for Valik.
Empowered. While wearing this ring, gain +2 Strength (maximum 20).
Numb. While wearing this ring, gain resistance to Cold damage and immunity to the Paralyzed condition.

Cleansing Cold(1/Long Rest). You can use your reaction when you take damage to send a wave of freezing cold energy through your body. The damage is reduced by an amount equal to 2d6 + {{constitution:modifier}}.
A ring once worn by the drow known as Malagar Virnn. Equipping this ring provides the wearer with Darkvision up to 120ft.

This ring has 3 charges, recharging 1 use at the end of every month. The wearer can expend this charge to summon Malagar within 5ft. as a shadow of their former selves to aid them. This shadow is incapable of speech but understands Sylvan and Infernal. After 3 minutes, the shadow evaporates. If all 3 charges are expended, the wearer can sacrifice the rings powers to summon them for a 4th time that month, causing the ring to no longer recharge.
Requires attunement by a Spellcaster.
Magicae Nocte. When you are attuned to this staff you receive a +2 bonus to your spell attack bonus and your spell save DC.

Dark Mists. This staff holds 4 charges, as a bonus action you may expend any number of charges to cast the spell Misty Step. For each charge you expend above one, the range of the spell increases by 30ft. The staff regains 1d4 charges at midnight.

Wave of Discord. As an action you can unleash a wave of dark energy in a 20ft. cone. All creatures in the area have to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save they take 8d6 Necrotic damage and have disadvantage on all saving throws and attack rolls until the end of their next turn. On a successful save they only take half damage. You can use this feature once per long rest.
A delicate ring adorned in curved grooves and topped by a jewel lapidated in the shape of a shield. It covers the wearer in a protective bearer.
While attuned to this ring, you have a +1 bonus to AC. Additionally, the ring has 4 charges which can be spent on the following ways:
Attentive Ward (1 charge).If you're ever surprised at the beginning of combat, you may use your reaction to spend a charge and act normally.
Reactive Ward (1 charge).At the beginning of combat, you can spend 1 charge to have advantage on the initiative roll.

Underhand Ward (1 charge).If you make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw, you can use your reaction to spend 1 charge and add your Intelligence modifier to the roll. You can do so after rolling, but before the DM tells you if it's a success or a failure.
The ring recovers all charges at the end of a long rest.

A dark blade whose edge fades into softly billowing smoke and embers. You gain +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with this magic weapon. This weapon has 3 charges and regains all charges after a long rest.

Smoking Shank. While holding this weapon you can spend a charge to cast the Misty Step spell, vanishing into a puff of black smoke. If you teleport within 5ft. of another creature you can make an attack against it with this weapon as part of the spell.
Where There's Fire. If you take 10 or more fire damage from a single source you can use your reaction to channel the fire into this blade and have this weapon regain a charge.

A spear burning with the radiance and heat of the sun. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with this magic weapon. This weapon has 5 charges and regains all charges after a long rest.
Angelic Language. You can speak, read, and write Celestial.
Celestial Resistance. You have resistance to fire and radiant damage.
Divine Judgement. You Wisdom score becomes 22, unless it is already 22 or higher.
Feathered Wings. You sprout a beautiful pair of fiery wings that grant you a flying speed of 45 feet and the ability to hover.

Scorching Radiance. While holding this weapon, any creature that you consider an enemy that comes within 10ft. of the spear is set aflame, taking an additional 1d6 radiant damage at the start of its turn. This effect ends when the creature is no longer within 10ft. of the spear. Enemies that would regain hitpoints while under the effect of the spear must pass a DC 23 Constitution Saving Throw, healing for half of the original health points on a success, and none on a failure.
Sheltering Light. While attuned to this weapon, you learn the Sheltering Light spell, and can cast it by consuming 1 charge of this weapon.
Radiating Confidence. While in direct sunlight, you cannot be surprised.

Returning Weapon. While attuned to this weapon and using it for a ranged attack, you can call it back in a straight line as a free action to have it return to your hand, dealing 1d6 radiant damage to all creatures in its path of return.

A dark blade whose edge fades into softly billowing smoke and embers. This weapon has 6 charges and regains 1d6 charges at dawn, you must have a Strength score of 16+ to use this weapon. It has the additional properties:

Detect Wealth. As an action, while holding the sword, you can point this weapon up into the air to detect huge monetary value. Until the next minute, the sword points toward the concentration of valuables within 300ft. that has the highest collective worth. This sword doesn't take into account magic items, and it can't point towards a concentration that is in an antimagic field or is protected from divination magic, as through nondetection.

Draconic Fear. As an action, while holding the sword, you can expend one charge, producing an ancient dragon's roar. Each creature of choice within 30ft. that can hear it must make a DC {{bahamut:dc}} Wisdom Save. Failure: Creature becomes frightened until the end of your next turn. While frightened in this way, a creature must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest available route on its turns.
Dragons Warmth. The sword grows warm when you are 1200ft. or closer to a dragon.
(2/Day) The wearer of this necklace can cast either "Blade Ward" or "True Strike" as a bonus action.

A blade forged with the essence of the 9 realms, wielded by Feyra in spite of the Gods to maintain order above all. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You may also cast Absorb Elements twice without a spell slot between long rests.
Adaptive Weapon. This weapons damage type is always treated as a targets vulnerability if it has one.

While holding this magic weapon, you gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it, and you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your druid and ranger spells. Whenever you take the attack action during your turn with this weapon, you can make its shaft grow like a branch, extending your reach by 5ft.

In addition, this weapon holds 4 charges. As a bonus action, you can expend a charge and cause cherry blossoms to whirl around you and follow your strikes until the start of your next turn. While in this state, attacks against you are made with disadvantage and you add this weapon's bonus to your AC and all your saving throws. After completing a long rest, this weapon restores 1d4 charges.

Gluttonous Fire. This weapon deals an additional 1d6 fire damage. In addition, this weapon has 6 charges, and regains 1d6 charges at dawn each day. You can expend charges to cast the spell Searing Smite (DC 16) at a level equal to the number of expended charges, for a maximum equal to your proficiency bonus.
Cooking Utensils. You can use this weapon as a sauce pan, requiring no fire to cook, and when you use it you add 1d6 to your skill checks made to cook food.

Cannibal Strike. If a creature dies while affected by this weapon's Searing Smite spell, it is instantly cooked, edible and delicious (this effect does not work on elementals, undead, construct, on tiny creatures and on creatures that are immune to fire). As an action, a creature can harvest and eat a ration of that dead creature, healing 1d6. These rations lose their healing magic after 1 hour, but remain edible. The amount of rations a creature can harvest from a cooked creature depends on its size: small (2), medium (3), huge (6), gargantuan (12).
You gain a bonus of 2 to your attack and damage rolls made with this weapon based on its rarity. This weapon inflicts force damage instead of slashing or piercing damage.

Ghostly Image. As a bonus action while holding this blade, you can cast the spell Mirror Image. While you cast the spell using this feature, you gain a bonus to your armor class equal to the number of duplicates you have active. In addition, the AC of your duplicates increases by 2. Once you have used this feature, you can't do so again until the next dawn.

Spirit Fighters. Whenever you take the attack action with this blade while you have at least 1 duplicate, you can cause the duplicate to make one attack. The duplicate uses your stats and attacks a creature within your reach, dealing force damage on a hit equal to this weapon's damage. When a duplicate hits a creature with an attack, it is destroyed.

Echoes of Death. Whenever you roll a critical hit with this weapon, you summon 1 duplicate as per the Ghostly Image feature. Duplicates created this way last for 1 minute or until they are destroyed, and you can't have more than 3 duplicates at a time.
A potion common to Bards and Minstrels. Dancer's Grace gives the drinker advantage on performance checks for 8 hours.
After drinking, gain the following effects for 1 hour:
Poisoned. You are poisoned.
Puke Fire. As an action, you can puke burning bile in a 15ft. line. All creatures caught in this line must make a DC 13 Dexterity Saving Throw or suffer fire damage equal to your Constitution Score.

A Toast to the Dead. You may raise your glass and speak the name of someone who has died. Upon drinking, a silent ghostly illusion of the named individual appears before you. The detail and clarity of this illusion is determined by how well you knew this person in life. Strangers appear as a blurry wisp of spectral vapour.
For three hours after drinking, you have +2 to all Strength based skill checks. At will, you can also let out a deafening belch that can be heard up to 1000 ft. away.
Your skin glitters and glows fabulously, for the next 8 hours, you may cast Faerie Fire once.
For six hours after drinking, you reduce all physical damage received by 2.
